title: Water-treatment decision hierarchy
summary: Prefer sealed commercial water, then officially verified supplies, then an official household-treatment method suitable for the known hazard.
content: Prefer sealed commercial water, then officially verified supplies, then an official household-treatment method suitable for the known hazard. When chemical, radiological, fuel, salt, or industrial contamination is suspected, seek a different source and official advice. Do not assume boiling removes chemicals, salt, fuel, or radioactive material.
materials: Notebook or printed card. Context: “Water-treatment decision hierarchy”.
Relevant labels, contacts, and official alerts — for «Water-treatment decision hierarchy»
steps: Prefer sealed commercial water, then officially verified supplies, then an official household-treatment method suitable for the known hazard.
When chemical, radiological, fuel, salt, or industrial contamination is suspected, seek a different source and official advice.
Assign one person to act and another to verify the result for Water-treatment decision hierarchy.
Record time, source, action, result, unresolved risk, and next review for Water-treatment decision hierarchy.
warnings: Do not assume boiling removes chemicals, salt, fuel, or radioactive material.
Official instructions and trained responders override this offline card for Water-treatment decision hierarchy.
common_mistakes: Acting before location, time, hazard, and responsible person are clear for Water-treatment decision hierarchy.
Failing to update the plan when conditions change during Water-treatment decision hierarchy.
alternatives: Use a paper note, SMS, radio message, or trusted messenger when the main system is unavailable for Water-treatment decision hierarchy.
failure_signs: The decision source, responsible person, or next review cannot be identified for Water-treatment decision hierarchy.
The action creates a new hazard or delays qualified help during Water-treatment decision hierarchy.
when_not_to_use: Do not use this card to override an evacuation order, emergency responder, clinician, product label, or local law for Water-treatment decision hierarchy.
short_term: For Water-treatment decision hierarchy, stabilize the immediate risk, communicate the decision, and schedule the next check before attention moves elsewhere.
long_term: After Water-treatment decision hierarchy, update supplies, contact lists, maps, training, and the written plan from what actually happened.
if_method_fails: Stop Water-treatment decision hierarchy, isolate the unsafe result, communicate the failure, and use the safest official or professionally supported fallback available.
environment_notes: Adapt Water-treatment decision hierarchy to Greek heat, wildfire smoke, earthquakes, islands, mountain roads, apartment stairs, power cuts, water interruptions, mobility limits, and official alerts.
sources: CDC — Water, sanitation and hygiene emergencies: https://www.cdc.gov/water-emergency/index.html
CDC — Keep food safe after an emergency: https://www.cdc.gov/food-safety/foods/keep-food-safe-after-emergency.html
American Red Cross — First aid steps: https://www.redcross.org/take-a-class/first-aid/performing-first-aid/first-aid-steps
